name: Thomas Dart
bio: Thomas J. Dart has served as Cook County Sheriff since 2006 — a 20-year incumbency. He previously served in the Illinois Senate (1991–1993) and Illinois House (1993–2003). He holds a BA from Providence College and a JD from Loyola University Chicago. He runs the second-largest single-site jail in the United States, overseeing ~6,700 employees and a $700M+ annual budget. In 2026, he faces no Democratic primary opponent.

jacket.note: ILSBE committee #19319. Top-25 donors scraped via Firecrawl 2026-03-14.
jacket.donors_note: Showing top 5 donors ($15,000 — 3.9% of total raised). Full contributor list: illinoissunshine.org/committees/19319/

red_flags.label: 2023: Deadliest jail year in 30+ years — 18 detainee deaths
red_flags.detail: Injustice Watch investigation found 18 detainee deaths at Cook County Jail in 2023, the deadliest year in at least 30 years. Supervision and medical care lapses were identified in at least half the deaths. Dart failed to properly report circumstances to state regulators and families.

red_flags.label: DOJ Consent Decree 2010–2017 — unconstitutional jail conditions
red_flags.detail: The federal DOJ sued Cook County in 2010 (United States v. Cook County, 1:10-cv-02946) over unconstitutional living conditions at Cook County Jail. A consent decree placed the jail under federal oversight for 7+ years. The decree was lifted in 2017. The 'cross-watching' practice (one officer monitoring two tiers) that was central to the decree is reportedly still in use.

red_flags.label: Unlawful detention class action — Black Chicagoans held weeks after bond paid
red_flags.detail: A class action lawsuit alleges Dart's office unlawfully detained nine Black Chicagoans for up to two weeks after they paid bond, violating the Fourth Amendment by refusing to release them to electronic monitoring. A federal appellate court allowed the suit to proceed in January 2024.

red_flags.label: Political coercion — 30+ employees forced onto 2022 campaign
red_flags.detail: Two senior jail officials (Drake Carpenter and Aracelis Gotay) filed suit alleging they were coerced into performing 2022 campaign work — checking petitions and canvassing — and were allegedly fired 8 days after amending their lawsuit to add the coercion allegation. According to the lawsuit, sign-in sheets show 30+ employees at campaign signature challenge sessions on county time. These allegations have not been adjudicated and Dart has not been found liable.

red_flags.label: Sgt. Pagani whistleblower suit (2025) — ghost pay, COVID fraud, nepotism
red_flags.detail: According to a 60-page federal lawsuit filed by Sgt. Nicole Pagani in 2025, she alleges she was demoted after reportedly discovering: ghost payrolling by 20+ employees; falsified COVID hazardous-duty pay records (potential federal fund misappropriation); nepotism in hiring (family members of senior staff); employees training personal dogs on county time; racist images on government devices; and further campaign coercion. She further alleges Dart's inner circle directed her to suppress the findings. These are unverified allegations in active litigation; Dart has not been charged or found liable.

red_flags.label: FBI ghost payroll probe (2022–2024)
red_flags.detail: The FBI joined an internal investigation of Cook County Sheriff's Office employees allegedly working second jobs at a Des Plaines security firm while on the county clock. FBI confirmed in late 2023 that no federal charges were filed, but the internal investigation concluded with firings.

red_flags.label: Skipped County Board hearing on jail deaths
red_flags.detail: In December 2024, the Cook County Board called a special hearing in response to Injustice Watch's reporting on the 18 jail deaths. Dart did not attend while families of deceased detainees gave tearful testimony.

red_flags.label: 100+ Director titles — patronage machine at county jail
red_flags.detail: A February 2026 report by C4 Cook County claimed to document over 100 'Director' and 'Executive Director' titles at Cook County Jail — reportedly more than entire state DOC systems. The report alleges the office has a revolving door of former journalists hired as communications staff, potentially creating structural conflicts of interest in critical coverage. The claim that this amounts to deliberate suppression has not been independently verified.

red_flags.label: Hired journalists to suppress coverage — ABC7, Tribune, WBBM
red_flags.detail: According to a report by C4 Cook County, Dart allegedly hired an ABC 7 reporter (now PIO), a Chicago Tribune reporter (now Executive Director), a WBBM producer (PIO), and others including Fox 32 anchor Dane Placko's son and radio host Roe Conn. These reported hires are from news organizations that cover Cook County. Whether these hiring decisions constitute intentional suppression of coverage has not been independently verified or adjudicated.

red_flags.label: Deborah Mell (Blagojevich family) hired as director
red_flags.detail: According to a report by C4 Cook County, Dart allegedly hired Deborah Mell — daughter of political figure Richard Mell and sister-in-law of impeached Governor Rod Blagojevich — as a director-level employee at the Sheriff's Office. This claim has not been independently verified, and no impropriety in the hiring has been established.

red_flags.detail: Electronic monitoring program sunset in 2024 following years of civil rights criticism — advocates called it a 'human rights crisis' with arbitrary restrictions on liberty

key_votes.bill: 2008: Suspended all foreclosure evictions in Cook County during subprime mortgag
key_votes.summary: 2008: Suspended all foreclosure evictions in Cook County during subprime mortgage crisis, citing harm to renters — Time magazine named him one of 100 Most Influential People (2009)

key_votes.bill: 2013: Launched Office of Mental Health Policy & Advocacy — 24-hour Care Line for
key_votes.summary: 2013: Launched Office of Mental Health Policy & Advocacy — 24-hour Care Line for mentally ill ex-inmates, screening all pre-bond detainees; pioneered diversion-over-incarceration approach

key_votes.bill: 2019: Filed lawsuit against Craigslist to close 'erotic services' section — dism
key_votes.summary: 2019: Filed lawsuit against Craigslist to close 'erotic services' section — dismissed on Section 230 immunity grounds

key_votes.bill: 2020-2024: Ran nation's largest single-site jail (Cook County), reduced populati
key_votes.summary: 2020-2024: Ran nation's largest single-site jail (Cook County), reduced population through electronic monitoring — program overseen 1989-2025

key_votes.bill: 2024 Nov: Cook County Board voted to sunset Dart's electronic monitoring program
key_votes.summary: 2024 Nov: Cook County Board voted to sunset Dart's electronic monitoring program, transferring oversight to Chief Judge amid human rights concerns about restrictive conditions

key_votes.bill: 2025 Mar: EM program formally transferred to Chief Judge's office; Dart's office
key_votes.summary: 2025 Mar: EM program formally transferred to Chief Judge's office; Dart's office offered 100 EM unit employees transition positions

key_votes.bill: 2006-present: Refused to honor ICE detainer requests — Cook County does not hold
key_votes.summary: 2006-present: Refused to honor ICE detainer requests — Cook County does not hold immigrants for ICE beyond release date; sanctuary jurisdiction

endorsements.significance: Official party slate endorsee — effectively party-backed machine candidate
Largest confirmed donor ($10,000). Operating engineers union.

jail_timeline.event: Dart elected Cook County Sheriff, defeating incumbent Michael Sheahan's chosen successor
DOJ files federal lawsuit — 'unconstitutional living conditions' at Cook County Jail
DOJ consent decree lifted after 7+ years of federal oversight
FBI joins internal ghost payrolling probe — employees allegedly working second jobs on county time
18 detainee deaths — deadliest year in 30+ years. Dart fails to notify regulators or families.
Cory Ulmer beaten to death by guards. Dart initially calls it 'a medical emergency.' 73-page federal civil rights suit filed.
County Board calls hearing on jail deaths. Dart does not attend.
Sgt. Pagani files 60-page federal whistleblower suit — ghost pay, COVID fraud, nepotism, racist images, campaign coercion
Running unopposed. Again. 20th year in office.

oversight_bodies.name: Cook County Board of Commissioners
oversight_bodies.role: Controls the Sheriff's Office budget. Has subpoena power for oversight hearings.
oversight_bodies.action: Find your County Board district commissioner and ask where they stood on jail oversight.

oversight_bodies.name: Illinois Attorney General
oversight_bodies.role: Can investigate civil rights violations in state-funded institutions.
oversight_bodies.action: Contact the IL Attorney General's office to request a civil rights investigation.

oversight_bodies.name: Illinois Prisoner Review Board
oversight_bodies.role: Required to receive reports on deaths in custody — reports Dart allegedly failed to file.
oversight_bodies.action: File a complaint with the IL Prisoner Review Board.

what_you_can_do: Read Injustice Watch's full 'Dying on Dart's Watch' series — the most complete record of jail deaths.
Contact your Cook County Board commissioner and ask them to call another oversight hearing.
Share this page — Dart's power depends on civic disengagement.
Register to vote in the November 2026 general election, when independent candidates may appear on the ballot.

read_more.label: Injustice Watch: Dying on Dart's Watch

career_history.highlight: 20-year incumbency; runs the second-largest single-site jail in the U.S.

policy_platform.position: Has resisted calls for large-scale decarceration; opposes elimination of cash bail without alternatives. Has framed jail conditions as a mental-health crisis requiring more resources, not fewer detainees.
Expanded electronic monitoring as an alternative to detention, but faces lawsuits alleging unlawful detention of people who paid bond.
Has publicly opposed Pornhub and payment processors facilitating gun straw purchases. Supports background check legislation.

social_pulse.summary: Predominantly negative coverage. Jail death investigations dominate social discourse around Dart. Supporters credit him with expanding diversion programs; critics cite 20+ years of jail deaths and federal investigations.
